policy.approved_by_policy: Only exact RustSec IDs may be ignored. Every exception must have owner, approval source, expiry, mitigation, and follow-up evidence. New, expired, unused, or undocumented advisories fail CI.
policy.approval_source: 2026-05-27 product owner instruction to enforce best-practice audit policy while allowing only explicit, reviewed exceptions

exceptions.reason: Transitive Linux GTK3 binding pulled through Tauri/wry/webkit2gtk; SignalOS does not depend on this crate directly and upstream migration is required.
exceptions.mitigation: Keep the exception time-boxed, track Tauri/wry GTK stack upgrades, and rerun cargo audit before each release.
exceptions.follow_up: Renewal review 2026-06-25: tauri 2.11.3 (latest) still pins gtk ^0.18, so GTK3 stack remains upstream-blocked. Track Tauri GTK4 migration roadmap; drop exception once tauri ships a non-gtk-0.18 path.

exceptions.reason: Transitive Linux GTK3 system binding pulled through Tauri/wry/webkit2gtk; SignalOS does not depend on this crate directly and upstream migration is required.

exceptions.reason: Transitive macro helper pulled by keyring through secret-service/zbus; this is an unmaintained warning, not a reported vulnerability, and requires upstream replacement.
exceptions.mitigation: Track keyring/secret-service/zbus updates and remove the exception as soon as the dependency path no longer requires derivative.
exceptions.follow_up: Renewal review 2026-06-25: keyring 4.x replaced the derivative-using path (now uses keyring-core + native backend stores). Upgrade keyring 2.3.3 -> 4.x in a focused PR to retire this exception before next renewal.

exceptions.reason: Transitive compatibility crate remains in Cargo.lock through upstream dependencies; this is an unmaintained warning, not a reported vulnerability.
exceptions.mitigation: Track dependency updates and remove the exception as soon as the dependency path no longer requires instant.
exceptions.follow_up: Renewal review 2026-06-25: not yet traced. Run `cargo tree -i instant` before next renewal to identify the still-blocking parent crate; many crates have moved to `web-time`.

exceptions.reason: Transitive proc-macro helper remains in Cargo.lock through upstream crates; this is an unmaintained warning, not a reported runtime vulnerability.
exceptions.mitigation: Track dependency updates and remove the exception as soon as the dependency path no longer requires proc-macro-error.
exceptions.follow_up: Renewal review 2026-06-25: not yet traced. Run `cargo tree -i proc-macro-error` before next renewal; many crates have migrated to `proc-macro-error2`.

exceptions.reason: Transitive Unicode helper pulled through Tauri urlpattern support; this is an unmaintained warning, not a reported vulnerability.
exceptions.mitigation: Track Tauri urlpattern dependency updates and remove the exception when the unic crate path disappears or is replaced.
exceptions.follow_up: Renewal review 2026-06-25: tauri-utils 2.9.3 (latest) still depends on urlpattern ^0.3 which pulls the unic crates. Track urlpattern releases or Tauri urlpattern replacement; drop exception once the unic transitive dep is gone.

exceptions.reason: Transitive Linux GTK3 binding pulled through Tauri/wry/webkit2gtk; the fixed glib line is not compatible with the current upstream GTK3 dependency path.
exceptions.mitigation: Treat this as the highest-risk exception, keep it time-boxed, monitor Tauri/wry GTK dependency updates, and block any new unsound advisory unless separately approved.
exceptions.follow_up: Renewal review 2026-06-25: tauri 2.11.3 (latest) still pins gtk ^0.18 which transitively requires glib 0.18.x. Highest-priority exception to retire alongside the GTK3->GTK4 migration.
